frontier_justification: This direction remains a May 2026 frontier because BCIs are moving from controlled demonstrations toward clinically useful motor and speech restoration, while noninvasive and semi-invasive neural signals remain unstable across subjects, sessions, and tasks. Foundation-model pretraining is promising, but plug-and-play decoding with minimal calibration is unresolved.

papers.title: Neuronal ensemble control of prosthetic devices by a human with tetraplegia
papers.key_contribution: This landmark BrainGate paper showed that intracortical recordings from motor cortex in a person with tetraplegia could be used to control external devices, including a computer cursor and simple prosthetic actions. It established the feasibility of human intracortical BCI for motor intention decoding.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Can neural activity recorded directly from human motor cortex be translated into useful control signals for assistive devices after paralysis?
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Implanted intracortical microelectrode arrays recorded ensemble spiking activity, which was mapped to cursor and device control using population decoding methods.
idea_genome.observation_genome: A human participant with tetraplegia achieved real-time control of external devices from cortical activity alone.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Performance was limited, calibration-heavy, and demonstrated in a small-scale setting with relatively simple decoding and task structure.

papers.title: Reach and grasp by people with tetraplegia using a neurally controlled robotic arm
papers.key_contribution: This BrainGate study extended intracortical BCI from basic cursor control to multi-degree-of-freedom robotic arm control for reach-and-grasp tasks in people with tetraplegia. It demonstrated more naturalistic motor decoding and functional object interaction.

idea_genome.problem_genome: How can intracortical BCIs move beyond simple cursor control to restore richer arm and hand function?
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Motor cortical population activity from implanted arrays was decoded into continuous robotic arm kinematics and grasp commands for online control.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Participants used neural activity to control a robotic arm to reach, grasp, and manipulate objects in 3D space.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: The system still required substantial supervised calibration and did not solve long-term robustness or broad generalization across sessions.

papers.title: EEGNet: a compact convolutional neural network for EEG-based brain-computer interfaces
papers.key_contribution: EEGNet introduced a compact deep neural architecture tailored to EEG BCI signals, using depthwise and separable convolutions to capture temporal and spatial structure efficiently. It became a widely used template for deep-learning-based neural decoding under limited-data conditions.

idea_genome.problem_genome: How can neural decoding improve when hand-engineered features and classical classifiers struggle to capture complex EEG structure across BCI paradigms?
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: A compact CNN with temporal convolutions followed by depthwise spatial filtering and separable convolutions learned task-relevant EEG representations end-to-end.
idea_genome.observation_genome: The model achieved competitive or superior performance across multiple EEG BCI paradigms while remaining parameter-efficient.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: It focused on noninvasive EEG and relatively modest-scale supervised decoding, leaving open transfer, scaling, and applicability to intracortical high-bandwidth signals.

papers.title: An integrated brain-machine interface platform with thousands of channels
papers.key_contribution: This Neuralink paper presented the N1-style high-channel-count implanted BCI platform concept, including flexible electrode threads, a neurosurgical robot, and custom electronics for large-scale neural recording. It shifted attention from decoding alone to scalable neural interface hardware needed for future high-capacity BCIs.

idea_genome.problem_genome: How can implanted BCIs scale channel count and surgical practicality to support richer decoding and long-term clinical deployment?
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Flexible polymer threads, custom low-power electronics, and robotic insertion were combined into an integrated high-channel-count recording platform.
idea_genome.observation_genome: The paper demonstrated a path toward thousands-channel implanted recording systems rather than conventional lower-channel rigid-array setups.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: It was primarily a hardware platform paper and did not yet establish broad clinical decoding performance or long-term human outcomes.

papers.title: A high-performance speech neuroprosthesis
papers.key_contribution: This paper showed that intracortical recordings from speech-related cortex can drive a high-performance speech neuroprosthesis, decoding intended words from neural activity at unprecedented rates for a person with severe paralysis. It marked a major expansion of BCI motor decoding into clinically meaningful communication decoding.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Can implanted BCIs decode attempted speech rapidly and accurately enough to restore practical communication?
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Intracortical array recordings were decoded with modern neural sequence modeling and language-constrained output to map cortical activity to words in real time.
idea_genome.observation_genome: The system achieved substantially faster communication rates than prior speech BCIs, demonstrating practical vocabulary-level decoding from cortex.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: The approach remained participant-specific, data-intensive, and constrained by vocabulary/task design rather than fully open-ended speech generation.

papers.title: BrainBERT: Self-supervised representation learning for intracranial recordings
papers.key_contribution: BrainBERT proposed a self-supervised transformer-style pretraining framework for intracranial neural signals, aiming to learn transferable representations from large unlabeled neural datasets. It represents the shift from task-specific decoders toward foundation-model-style neural signal modeling.

idea_genome.problem_genome: How can neural decoders become more data-efficient and transferable across tasks, sessions, and subjects when labeled neural data are scarce?
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: A BERT-like self-supervised pretraining strategy was applied to intracranial neural time series to learn general-purpose latent representations before downstream fine-tuning.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Pretrained representations improved downstream decoding performance and data efficiency relative to training from scratch on limited labeled neural data.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Foundation-style neural models remain early, with unresolved questions about cross-subject generalization, scaling laws, multimodal alignment, and clinical reliability.

papers.title: Large Cognition Model: Towards Pretrained EEG Foundation Model
papers.key_contribution: Paper B introduces a pretrained EEG foundation model, the Large Cognition Model, designed to learn generalizable representations from large-scale heterogeneous EEG data for downstream brain analysis and BCI tasks.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Paper B addresses the lack of robust, transferable EEG models caused by limited labeled datasets and strong variability across subjects, sessions, and recording conditions.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Its core approach is to pretrain a large foundation model on diverse EEG data so it can capture broadly useful neural representations before being adapted to specific downstream tasks. This combines large-scale self-supervised or pretrained modeling ideas from foundation models with EEG signal analysis to improve generalization and data efficiency.
idea_genome.observation_genome: The paper reports that pretrained large-scale EEG representations improve performance and transfer across multiple downstream tasks and settings compared with task-specific training baselines.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Despite improved generalization, EEG foundation models still face challenges from data heterogeneity, limited standardization, and the gap between non-invasive EEG signals and high-fidelity neural decoding needed for complex cognition or speech.

edges.evolution_focus: The 2012 paper directly extends the original BrainGate human intracortical decoding paradigm from low-dimensional cursor/device control to higher-dimensional reach-and-grasp control of a robotic arm.

EEGNet branches from the broader BCI decoding problem by focusing on noninvasive EEG and replacing classical feature engineering with compact deep learning architectures tailored to neural time series.

The Neuralink platform combines the decoding ambitions of BCI research with a hardware-scaling agenda, arguing that richer deep decoders will require much larger, more practical implanted recording systems.

With higher-bandwidth implanted recording ecosystems in view, the field radiates from arm-movement decoding into speech decoding, where modern neural sequence decoders unlock a new communication application of intracortical BCI.

BrainBERT evolves beyond task-specific supervised speech/motor decoders by introducing self-supervised pretraining, aiming for reusable neural representations that can support many downstream BCI tasks.

The shift from A to B is a hybridization because the field moves from a specialized participant-specific invasive speech decoder to combining BCI/EEG analysis with foundation-model pretraining paradigms from mainstream AI to build broadly transferable neural models.

open_question: Large Cognition Model explores EEG foundation modeling, but plug-and-play BCI remains unsolved because noninvasive signals drift across sessions, tasks, and people and labels are sparse. What self-supervised neural foundation model would align EEG, MEG, ECoG, movement, and speech data across subjects and days to enable calibrated motor and speech decoding with minimal patient-specific calibration?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Neuroscience / Brain-Computer Interface.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 7 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: Large Cognition Model: Towards Pretrained EEG Foundation Model
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: EEG foundation models improve representation learning, but real BCI deployment still needs cross-subject, cross-session, cross-task transfer with minimal calibration and calibrated uncertainty.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a still-open limitation at the endpoint of this lineage, not a solved benchmark task.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
